title: Soap Film
description: A soap film stretched across a wire ring, coloured by real thin-film interference: the shader carries a live thickness field a few hundred nanometres deep — swirled by noise, draining upward until the near-invisible black film appears at the top — and for each of three wavelengths interferes the front- and back-surface reflections (I ∝ cos²(2π·n·d·cosθ/λ)). With Fresnel on top, the film is glassy face-on and mirror-bright at grazing, and the whole soap-bubble rainbow falls out of the physics. The film sags and ripples in 3D; drag to orbit the wand. Thickness, swirl, drainage, ripple, pattern scale and orbit speed are live and audio-mappable (loudness stirs the marbling, beats slap ring waves into the film).
